ingredients: 1 (1-inch) piece ginger, peeled and cut into matchsticks
6 scallions, sliced (white and green parts separated if desired)
4 (6-ounce) firm white fish fillets (such as striped bass or halibut)
Kosher salt and black pepper, to taste
2 tbsp Chinese rice wine or dry sherry
steps: Set a large steamer basket over a skillet of simmering water.
Scatter some ginger, garlic and scallions on a heatproof plate. Season fish with salt and pepper and place on the plate; top with remaining aromatics and snow peas.
Cover and steam until fish is just cooked through, about 8-12 minutes depending on thickness.
Mix sesame oil, sugar, soy sauce and rice wine; heat vegetable oil until hot.
Pour sauce over fish, then drizzle with hot oil to sizzle the aromatics; serve immediately.
tips: Do not oversteam; start checking at 8 minutes. The hot oil finish is what perfumes the dish, so heat it well but avoid smoking.
